No comms UI in apps/dentalemon → a Playwright e2e is impossible. ws.chat-room.integration.test.ts now boots the real app and proves the live auth chain REJECTS an unauthenticated/invalid-token WS upgrade with 401 (the config-shape test was blind to this). The ledger e2e flag remains because the test runs in the server lane, not Playwright. Authenticated connect + broadcast + onClose deferred.

CLOSED in Phase 3a by apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/portal-patient-self-service.spec.ts (logged-in patient drives /portal → appointments/bills/balance, RED-proven). Retained only because the deterministic e2e spec-index is grep-blind to this item (known FP) — drop when detection hardens.

e2e blocked (same OneSignal gating): usePushNotificationRouting only registers the click listener when isOneSignalEnabled(), which is false in the test env (placeholder AppID rejected — verified). Deep-link resolution IS covered by fe-unit (notification-routing.test.ts: booking→/calendar, recall→/patients, billing→/billing, unknown→/dashboard). A true e2e needs a real OneSignal app id — deferred, not faked.

e2e blocked in the test env (empirically verified): the OneSignal web SDK rejects the non-prod placeholder VITE_ONESIGNAL_APP_ID ("AppID doesn’t match existing apps"), so isOneSignalEnabled() stays false and the opt-in prompt never mounts. Component logic IS covered by fe-unit (push-opt-in-prompt.test.tsx: enable→requestPermission→dismiss + PUSH_OPT_IN_DISMISSED_KEY). A true e2e needs a real OneSignal app id (or a prod test-seam) — deferred, not faked.

NOT user-reachable: base /billing/invoices (POST/PATCH/pay) has zero dentalemon consumers — the product UI uses only /dental/billing/invoices (verified by grep; the patient-profile test matcher is lenient). findBillingParty (person↔billing merchant/customer existence) proven at contract (billing-lifecycle.hurl merchant/customer 404 validation) + be-unit (createInvoice.test.ts, markInvoiceUncollectible.test.ts). Backend-only boundary → endpoint orphan ratchet, not the user-facing e2e backlog.
